gene_region.label: OTOA transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical OTOA genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: OTOA encodes an auditory or vestibular sensory gene involved in hair-cell mechanotransduction, cochlear ionic balance, stereocilia structure, or ribbon-synapse signaling. This topic bundle emphasizes human sensory biology, with interpretation centered on visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling.
gene_context.clinical_context: The local OTOA bundle is oriented toward human sensory biology. Use it for pathway triage, phenotype review, and candidate-gene context; external variant classification is required before clinical interpretation.
variant_effect_overview: OTOA variants can alter, tag, or contextualize visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling.
Effect direction depends on exact allele class, dosage, tissue, ancestry, phenotype definition, and assay context.
condition_research_overview: Human senses, sensory transduction, and sensory-neuron research context
Vision, hearing, balance, smell, taste, touch, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoception pathway biology
Candidate-gene triage where sensory organ, receptor class, channel gating, tissue expression, and phenotype timing change interpretation
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: OTOA methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data.
methylation_effects: Promoter or first-exon methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local expression state for OTOA.
Because OTOA biology is tissue and pathway dependent, methylation values are context rather than a stand-alone biomarker.

Combined sequence plus methylation findings are most useful when interpreted against visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling.
methylation_condition_research: Epigenetic regulation studies that use OTOA promoter or gene-body methylation as expression-context evidence.

Variant-plus-methylation review for visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling.

biorender_visuals.label: OTOA biochemical figure starter
biorender_visuals.focus: OTOA: human sensory biology via visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling
biorender_visuals.usage_note: BioRender template and icon-library search results are bundled as figure starters. Open the template link in BioRender, then add or replace the listed icons to make a gene-specific biochemical pathway image.

gene_context.concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a OTOA human sensory biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

variant_records.common_name: OTOA human sensory biology pathway model
variant_records.clinical_interpretation: OTOA human-senses variation model is curated as a OTOA marker for Human senses, sensory transduction, and sensory-neuron research context, Vision, hearing, balance, smell, taste, touch, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoception pathway biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.

functional_effects: Can modify or tag visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling.
Best interpreted with tissue, ancestry, phenotype, and assay context.
